author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
vada vada hari, chadma nā kariha,
vipade beḍhala deśa
e tattva jāniyā, āge palāola,
śravaṇa daśana keśa

tāra pāche pāche, locana vacana,
tārā dohe dila bhaṅga
mora mora kari, rātra⋅dine mari,
jamadūte dekhe raṅga

sundara nagare, prati ghare ghare,
viṣama jamera thānā
daṇḍa je divasa, vatsara gaṇiche,
kona dine dibe hānā

dārā⋅putra⋅vadhū, jatana kariche,
sakali nimera titā
maraṇa samaye, hāte gale bāndhi,
mukhe jvāli dibe citā

vadana bhariyā, hari nā baliyā,
śamana taribe kise
dāsa locana, kahiyā phāraka,
maricha āpana doṣe
en_translation:
Speak, speak the Name of Lord Hari! Do not pretend. The country is surrounded by calamity. Knowing this truth, they have already fled—your ears, your teeth, and your hair.

Behind them, your eyes and your speech have both broken their promise. Saying "mine, mine," you die day and night while the messengers of the God of Death see this as a show.

In this beautiful city, in every house, there is a terrible outpost of the God of Death. Counting the days and years of your punishment, on which day will he finally strike?

Your wife, sons, and daughters-in-law all take care of themselves; everything is as bitter as neem. At the time of your death, they will bind your hands and neck and light the funeral pyre at your mouth.

If you do not wholeheartedly chant the Name of Lord Hari, how will you ever cross over the messenger of death? Locana Dāsa says clearly: "You are dying only due to your own fault."
